index_title: Cloudflare Build Badge - Live Status Badges for Pages & Workers
index_description: Generate live build status badges for your Cloudflare Pages and Workers projects. Display your continuous deployment success seamlessly inside GitHub READMEs or websites.
seo_og_image_alt: Cloudflare Build Badge — live build status badges for Pages and Workers
configurator_description: Generate a custom badge URL for any repository connected to Cloudflare Workers Builds.
configurator_style_tooltip: Badge style. Possible values: flat, flat-square, plastic, for-the-badge, social.
configurator_logo_tooltip: Icon slug from simple-icons. You can click the icon title on simple-icons to copy the slug or they can be found in the slugs.md file in the simple-icons repository.
configurator_logo_color_tooltip: The color of the logo (hex, rgb, rgba, hsl, hsla and css named colors supported). Supported for simple-icons logos but not for custom logos.
configurator_logo_size_tooltip: Make icons adaptively resize by setting auto. Useful for some wider logos like amd and amg. Supported for simple-icons logos but not for custom logos.
configurator_label_tooltip: Override the default left-hand-side text (URL-Encoding needed for spaces or special characters!)
configurator_label_color_tooltip: Background color of the left part (hex, rgb, rgba, hsl, hsla and css named colors supported).
configurator_color_tooltip: Background color of the right part (hex, rgb, rgba, hsl, hsla and css named colors supported).
configurator_cache_seconds_tooltip: HTTP cache lifetime (rules are applied to infer a default value on a per-badge basis, any values specified below the default will be ignored).
configurator_customize_hint: The status color and message are computed automatically from the latest build; the rest of the look is up to you.
configurator_preview_placeholder: Enter a username and repository to generate a badge.
examples_description: These are real, live badges from this very project, shown in a few different Shields.io styles. Any Shields query parameter is supported.
examples_view_badge: Open the badge in a new tab
about_what_text: Cloudflare Build Badge is a Cloudflare Worker that generates SVG badges showing the build status of any repository deployed with Cloudflare Workers Builds, ready to embed in your README or website.
about_problem_text: GitHub doesn't ship a native status badge for Cloudflare Workers & Pages checks the way it does for GitHub Actions. This project fills that gap by querying the GitHub Checks API and translating the result into a Shields.io badge, without requiring any setup in your repository.
how_it_works_description: Every badge request goes through these four steps at the edge of Cloudflare's network.
how_it_works_step1_text: You request the badge URL for any GitHub repository connected to Cloudflare Workers Builds.
how_it_works_step2_text: The Worker queries the GitHub API for the latest Cloudflare Workers & Pages check on the requested branch.
how_it_works_step3_text: The check status is mapped to a Shields.io badge and rendered as an SVG image.
how_it_works_step4_text: The badge updates automatically every time the URL is requested again.
deploy_description: Start from the template and follow these steps to get your own instance running.
deploy_step2_label: 2. Push to GitHub and connect Cloudflare
deploy_step2_text: Push the repository to GitHub, connect it in Cloudflare Workers Builds, and ignore the first few failing builds while you finish the setup.
deploy_step3_text: Set your domain in "site" inside astro.config.mjs and update the worker name in wrangler.jsonc.
deploy_step4_text_a: Option A: keep "route.pattern" to use a custom domain.
deploy_step4_text_b: Option B: remove "route" and set "workers_dev: true" to use a workers.dev subdomain.
deploy_step5_text: Create a classic GitHub token with read-only "repo" access and store it with Wrangler:
